title: Scalloped Fruit Bowl with Sunflowers
description:
We call this shape a capovettica — a wide, open bowl with a scalloped rim that fans out like petals. Seen from above, it almost looks like a sunflower itself, which fits perfectly because that's exactly what our artisans have painted on it. Three large sunflowers with brown centres and yellow petals sit among green leaves and clusters of small red flowers, all painted freehand on a background that shifts from warm yellow at the rim to deep blue in the centre.

The scalloped edge gives the bowl personality. Each lobe curves outward slightly, and you'll notice blue finger-shaped indentations along the rim where the artisan pinched and shaped the clay while it was still soft. It's a detail that reminds you this was made by hand, not pressed from a mould.

At the table, it's great for fruit — oranges, lemons, figs look beautiful sitting in it. It also works for serving salad, bread, or just about anything you want to present nicely. When it's not holding food, it sits happily on a sideboard or kitchen shelf as a decorative piece.

Made in Vietri sul Mare using food-safe glazes. Each bowl is painted individually, so expect small differences in the placement of the flowers and the exact shade of the colours.
excerpt: A scalloped ceramic fruit bowl with hand-painted sunflowers on yellow and blue. Shaped and painted freehand in Vietri sul Mare.
seoTitle: Sunflower Fruit Bowl — Ceramiche Da Mario
seoDescription: Handmade scalloped ceramic fruit bowl with sunflowers, painted freehand by artisans in Vietri sul Mare. From Ceramiche Da Mario in Praiano.
